title: atractylis arbuscula subsp. arbuscula
intro: Atractylis arbuscula subsp. arbuscula, also known as Kusaboshi or Hare's Ear, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone content. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to provide relief from pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, thereby promoting overall health and well-being. Atractylis arbuscula subsp. arbuscula is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including colds, flu, and digestive issues.
